checklist_item.title: Verify Fork Digest Computation: Is compute_fork_digest correctly implemented to derive the expected digest for each epoch?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with arbitrary fork digests. If fork digest validation is missing, blocks from different fork versions could be processed as if they belong to the current fork.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the compute_fork_digest function implementation. 2. Verify it takes the block epoch as input and derives the correct fork version for that epoch. 3. Confirm the genesis validators root is correctly incorporated into the digest computation. 4. Test with known test vectors: compute digest for pre-fork epoch, at-fork epoch, and post-fork epoch. 5. Verify the function handles epoch boundary conditions correctly (e.g., exact fork epoch transition).
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-50, Element: SG-003. Internal verification that the fork digest computation is correct, as incorrect computation would allow fork confusion attacks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does the CL implement timeout handling for Engine API calls when EL returns SYNCING status?

reachability.notes: A buggy EL that returns SYNCING indefinitely would stall the CL if no timeout is implemented. Not directly attacker-controlled but could be triggered by triggering EL sync issues via P2P.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all Engine API call sites in the CL codebase that interact with the EL. 2. Review timeout configuration for each Engine API method (engine_newPayloadV*, engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV*, etc.). 3. Verify that explicit timeout values are set and are reasonable (not indefinite). 4. Test behavior when EL returns SYNCING status repeatedly - confirm CL does not block indefinitely. 5. Verify that other consensus duties (attestations, block proposals) continue to be processed even when Engine API calls are timing out. 6. Check for proper error handling and retry logic when timeouts occur.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-51, Trust Boundaries: tb-006, tb-007, Threat: stride-014

checklist_item.title: Verify CL resilience: Can the CL continue processing attestations and block proposals while Engine API calls are pending or timing out?

reachability.notes: A buggy EL that returns SYNCING indefinitely would stall the CL if no timeout is implemented. Not directly attacker-controlled but could be triggered by triggering EL sync issues via P2P.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review the CL's task/thread architecture to understand how Engine API calls are dispatched. 2. Verify that Engine API communication is performed asynchronously or in a dedicated thread/task. 3. Check that the main consensus loop is not blocked by Engine API calls. 4. Test by simulating a slow/unresponsive EL and verify attestation duties continue. 5. Verify block production responsibilities are not indefinitely delayed by EL sync issues. 6. Review logging and alerting mechanisms for prolonged SYNCING states.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-51, Covered Actors: actor-consensus-layer, actor-execution-engine

checklist_item.title: Verify invariant: Extended SYNCING status from EL does not cause CL to miss attestation or proposal duties

reachability.notes: A buggy EL that returns SYNCING indefinitely would stall the CL if no timeout is implemented. Not directly attacker-controlled but could be triggered by triggering EL sync issues via P2P.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Design a test scenario where EL returns SYNCING status for an extended period (e.g., 5+ minutes). 2. Attempt to falsify the invariant by observing whether CL misses any scheduled attestation duties during this period. 3. Attempt to falsify by checking if block proposal opportunities are lost when CL is the designated proposer. 4. Verify state transitions: CL should transition to a degraded operational mode rather than complete stall. 5. Check for any race conditions between Engine API timeout handling and consensus duty scheduling. 6. Verify that once EL returns to VALID/INVALID status, normal operations resume without manual intervention.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-51, Primary Element: SG-002, Type: invariant

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-005/tb-009: Is the proposer deterministically identifiable and cryptographically bound to each slot?

reachability.notes: If the lookahead is incorrect, the wrong validator could be blamed or slashed for actions they did not take.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify that state.proposer_lookahead is computed deterministically from beacon state. 2. Confirm that the proposer lookahead computation is identical across all conforming implementations. 3. Validate that the block proposer signature cryptographically binds the proposer identity to the block. 4. Attempt to craft a block where the proposer signature does not match the expected proposer from the lookahead and verify rejection. 5. Check that lookahead values cannot be manipulated after computation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-52, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-009, Threat: stride-013

checklist_item.title: Verify Proposer-Consensus Layer Interaction: Does the consensus layer correctly enforce proposer assignment from the lookahead?

reachability.notes: If the lookahead is incorrect, the wrong validator could be blamed or slashed for actions they did not take.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace the data flow from proposer lookahead computation to block proposal acceptance. 2. Verify that the consensus layer rejects blocks from validators not matching the lookahead for a given slot. 3. Check for race conditions or timing issues where lookahead updates could create ambiguity. 4. Confirm that slashing decisions correctly reference the proposer lookahead at the time of the alleged violation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-52, Node: actor-consensus-layer

checklist_item.title: Verify Validator Assignment Integrity: Can a validator deny or falsely claim proposer duty assignment?

reachability.notes: If the lookahead is incorrect, the wrong validator could be blamed or slashed for actions they did not take.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify that proposer lookahead is publicly auditable and deterministically reproducible. 2. Check that validators cannot influence their own selection in the lookahead. 3. Confirm that signature requirements prevent a validator from claiming another validator proposed a block. 4. Test that historical proposer assignments are immutable once computed. 5. Verify that any dispute resolution mechanism correctly identifies the assigned proposer.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-52, Nodes: actor-proposer, actor-validator

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD delay sufficient to prevent same-epoch randao manipulation of proposer assignments?

reachability.notes: A validator controlling multiple slots could attempt to bias the randao mix to manipulate future proposer assignments. The MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD provides a time buffer, but the attack is theoretically possible with sufficient validator share.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review the implementation of MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD delay in proposer selection. 2. Identify all randao mix contribution points at epoch boundaries. 3. Analyze the proposer lookahead calculation to verify it correctly incorporates the seed lookahead delay. 4. Model the attack surface: calculate the validator stake percentage required to bias randao outcomes. 5. Verify that the delay between randao contribution and proposer assignment prevents same-epoch manipulation. 6. Test edge cases at epoch boundaries where timing assumptions may be violated. 7. Check for any code paths that could allow early revelation or late contribution of randao values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-53, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Primary Element: SG-006. This is an invariant property covering the consensus layer boundary between validators and the randao system. Bug bounty eligible - requires demonstration that MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD is insufficient with specific validator stake percentage.

checklist_item.title: Supporting Node Check: Verify validator randao contributions cannot be withheld or replayed to influence proposer selection

reachability.notes: Supporting check for boundary integrity - focuses on validator node behavior.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review how validator randao contributions are collected and validated. 2. Verify that missing randao contributions are handled correctly (e.g., using a default value). 3. Check for replay protection on randao contributions. 4. Verify that the consensus layer properly rejects late or out-of-order randao values. 5. Test scenarios where a validator deliberately withholds their randao contribution to influence the mix.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-53, Supporting check for nodes: actor-validator, actor-consensus-layer

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Are all proposer indices from compute_proposer_indices validated against the active validator set?

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the compute_proposer_indices function and trace all validator index selection paths. 2. Verify that the function explicitly checks each selected index against the active validator set for the target epoch. 3. Test with edge cases: (a) validator that exits between lookahead computation and slot execution, (b) validator pending activation, (c) slashed validator still in registry. 4. Verify that the epoch parameter used for active set lookup matches the epoch for which proposers are being computed. 5. Check for race conditions where validator status could change during computation. 6. Attempt to construct a scenario where an inactive validator index is returned and verify the system handles this gracefully.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-35, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Source Invariant: FULU-SG004-INV-003, Threat: stride-010

checklist_item.title: Verify Consensus Layer Correctly Filters Exited Validators from Proposer Selection

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review the consensus layer's get_active_validator_indices function to verify it correctly excludes exited validators. 2. Trace how validator exit_epoch is checked against the current/target epoch. 3. Verify that validators with exit_epoch <= current_epoch are excluded. 4. Test boundary conditions: validator exits exactly at the epoch boundary. 5. Confirm that the active set used for proposer selection is computed atomically with no TOCTOU vulnerabilities.
checklist_item.notes: Supporting check for actor-consensus-layer. Source: PROP-W1B10-35

checklist_item.title: Verify Slashed Validators Cannot Be Selected as Block Proposers

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify how the slashed flag is checked during proposer selection. 2. Verify that compute_proposer_indices excludes validators where slashed == true. 3. Test scenario: validator gets slashed between lookahead computation and actual slot. 4. Verify that if a slashed validator somehow produces a block, it is rejected by honest nodes. 5. Check if there are any code paths that could allow a slashed validator to bypass this filter. 6. Review historical slashing events to ensure the exclusion logic is consistent with specification.
checklist_item.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator. A slashed validator producing blocks could create conflicting blocks. Source: PROP-W1B10-35

checklist_item.title: Verify Not-Yet-Activated Validators Are Excluded from Proposer Selection

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review how activation_epoch is checked in the proposer selection logic. 2. Verify that validators with activation_epoch > current_epoch are excluded from the active set. 3. Test edge case: validator activates exactly at the epoch for which proposers are being computed. 4. Check that the lookahead computation correctly uses the future epoch's active set, not the current one. 5. Verify no off-by-one errors in epoch comparisons. 6. Test with validators in the activation queue to ensure they cannot be selected prematurely.
checklist_item.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator. Source: PROP-W1B10-35

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-004: Are P2P block submissions with maximum blob commitments properly rate-limited to prevent resource exhaustion?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with maximum blob count. The dynamic limit from EIP-7892 could be higher than pre-Fusaka limits, stressing node resources. However, high-effort DoS attacks are out of scope.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the P2P block gossip handler and locate the rate-limiting logic for block proposals. 2. Verify that the rate limit enforces at most one block per slot per proposer. 3. Simulate a malicious peer sending multiple blocks with maximum blob commitments in the same slot from the same proposer identity. 4. Confirm that excess blocks are rejected and the peer is penalized or disconnected. 5. Measure memory and CPU usage when receiving blocks at the dynamically configured maximum blob limit (EIP-7892). 6. Verify that resource consumption remains bounded even under sustained attack.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-54, Trust Boundary: tb-004, Threat: stride-015. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation. High-effort DoS attacks are out of scope per bug bounty rules. Focus on low-effort attacks that could cause disproportionate resource consumption.

checklist_item.title: Verify P2P Peer Validation: Does the P2P layer properly authenticate and track proposer identity for rate-limiting purposes?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with maximum blob count. The dynamic limit from EIP-7892 could be higher than pre-Fusaka limits, stressing node resources. However, high-effort DoS attacks are out of scope.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review how proposer identity is extracted and validated from incoming P2P block messages. 2. Check if proposer identity can be spoofed or if rate-limiting can be bypassed by forging proposer IDs. 3. Verify that rate-limiting is applied per authenticated proposer, not per connection or IP. 4. Test whether a single malicious peer can masquerade as multiple proposers to circumvent rate limits. 5. Confirm cryptographic validation of proposer signatures before processing resource-intensive blob data.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-54, Supporting check for node: actor-p2p-peer. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Consensus Layer Resource Management: Does the consensus layer properly bound resource allocation for blob commitment validation?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with maximum blob count. The dynamic limit from EIP-7892 could be higher than pre-Fusaka limits, stressing node resources. However, high-effort DoS attacks are out of scope.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the consensus layer components that process incoming blocks with blob commitments. 2. Review memory allocation patterns when processing blocks at the dynamic blob limit. 3. Verify that blob KZG commitment validation is properly bounded in time and memory. 4. Check for any amplification attacks where a small attacker input causes disproportionate computation. 5. Test behavior when the dynamic blob limit (EIP-7892) is at its maximum configuration. 6. Verify graceful degradation under load rather than crash or hang conditions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-54, Supporting check for node: actor-consensus-layer. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-005: Are execution payloads with invalid blob commitment counts rejected before forwarding to EL?

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit blocks that pass early checks but fail blob count validation, wasting CL processing time. If the CL still forwards to the EL before checking blob count, it wastes additional EL resources.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the CL code path that validates incoming execution payloads from P2P/Consensus. 2. Locate the blob commitment count validation assertion. 3. Verify the assertion order: confirm blob count check occurs BEFORE any Engine API calls to EL. 4. Craft test payloads that pass parent_hash, prev_randao, and timestamp checks but have invalid blob commitment counts (e.g., count mismatch, overflow values). 5. Submit these payloads and verify they are rejected at the blob count assertion. 6. Monitor Engine API logs to confirm NO calls are made to EL for rejected payloads. 7. Measure CL processing time to assess DoS impact from repeated invalid submissions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-55, Trust Boundary: tb-005. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation to confirm exploitability and impact. The property is a post-condition ensuring ordered validation prevents resource waste.

checklist_item.title: Verify Consensus Layer (actor-consensus-layer) enforces blob count validation before EL interaction

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit blocks that pass early checks but fail blob count validation, wasting CL processing time. If the CL still forwards to the EL before checking blob count, it wastes additional EL resources.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review CL implementation for execution payload handling. 2. Trace the validation sequence from payload receipt to EL forwarding. 3. Verify blob commitment count is validated in the assertion chain. 4. Check for any code paths that could bypass blob count validation (e.g., fast-path optimizations, cached payloads). 5. Confirm the CL does not make Engine API newPayload calls for payloads failing blob count checks. 6. Test edge cases: zero blobs, maximum blobs, blob count at boundary values.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-55, Supporting node: actor-consensus-layer. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Proposer (actor-proposer) cannot cause CL to forward invalid blob count payloads

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit blocks that pass early checks but fail blob count validation, wasting CL processing time. If the CL still forwards to the EL before checking blob count, it wastes additional EL resources.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify how proposer-originated blocks enter the validation pipeline. 2. Verify that proposer blocks undergo the same blob count validation as P2P-received blocks. 3. Test if a malicious proposer can craft blocks that bypass blob count checks. 4. Verify that even locally-built blocks are validated before EL submission. 5. Check rate limiting and anti-spam measures for proposer-submitted blocks.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-55, Supporting node: actor-proposer. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: Requires further investigation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-002: Does the precompile correctly trigger out-of-gas revert when available_gas < 6900?

reachability.notes: A caller can deliberately provide exactly 6899 gas to trigger the out-of-gas revert. The behavior must be consistent with standard EVM out-of-gas handling.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the precompile gas check implementation at the EVM-precompile boundary (tb-002). 2. Call the precompile with exactly 6899 gas and verify it reverts with out-of-gas. 3. Verify that all remaining gas is consumed upon revert (standard EVM out-of-gas semantics). 4. Call with exactly 6900 gas and verify no revert occurs due to gas. 5. Test edge cases: 0 gas, 1 gas, 6898 gas, 6900 gas, 6901 gas. 6. Verify this is the ONLY condition under which the precompile reverts (no other revert paths exist). 7. Compare behavior across different client implementations for consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-58, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Source Invariant: EIP7951-SG006-INV-002, Source Assumption: asm-002

checklist_item.title: Verify EVM-to-Precompile Gas Consumption: Is remaining gas fully consumed on out-of-gas revert?

reachability.notes: A caller can deliberately provide exactly 6899 gas to trigger the out-of-gas revert. The behavior must be consistent with standard EVM out-of-gas handling.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct a transaction calling the precompile with gas < 6900. 2. After the revert, verify that gasUsed equals the gas provided (all gas consumed). 3. Check that no gas is refunded to the caller. 4. Verify consistency with other EVM out-of-gas behaviors (e.g., CALL with insufficient gas). 5. Test from nested call contexts to ensure gas consumption propagates correctly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-58, Supporting check for actor-evm node

checklist_item.title: Verify Precompile Revert Exclusivity: Does the precompile revert ONLY for gas < 6900?

reachability.notes: A caller can deliberately provide exactly 6899 gas to trigger the out-of-gas revert. The behavior must be consistent with standard EVM out-of-gas handling.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Review precompile implementation for all potential revert/error paths. 2. Verify that when gas >= 6900, no revert can occur regardless of input. 3. Test with malformed inputs, edge-case values, and boundary conditions with sufficient gas. 4. Confirm that input validation errors (if any) do NOT cause reverts but are handled per EIP specification. 5. Fuzz the precompile with various inputs while providing >= 6900 gas and verify no reverts.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-58, Supporting check for actor-precompile node

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-008: Is BLOB_SCHEDULE sorted in descending epoch order to ensure correct parameter lookup?

reachability.notes: Sorting direction is critical for correct schedule lookup. Descending sort finds the most recent applicable entry. Ascending sort would find the earliest entry, returning outdated parameters.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate the get_blob_parameters function and identify the BLOB_SCHEDULE data structure. 2. Verify that the schedule is sorted in DESCENDING order by epoch (highest epoch first). 3. Create test cases with multiple epoch entries and confirm the function returns the first entry where epoch >= entry.EPOCH. 4. Attempt to falsify by temporarily changing sort order to ascending and verify that incorrect (outdated) parameters are returned. 5. Compare sort direction implementation across different client implementations to ensure consensus compatibility. 6. Check for any edge cases: empty schedule, single entry, epoch boundary conditions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-59, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Primary Element: SG-001. This is a consensus-critical implementation detail - all clients must agree on sorting direction.

checklist_item.title: Verify Blob Schedule Consensus: Do all client implementations use identical sorting direction for BLOB_SCHEDULE lookup?

reachability.notes: Sorting direction is critical for correct schedule lookup. Descending sort finds the most recent applicable entry. Ascending sort would find the earliest entry, returning outdated parameters.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify all client implementations that process blob parameters (actor-blob-schedule, actor-consensus-layer). 2. For each implementation, locate the BLOB_SCHEDULE sorting logic. 3. Verify that ALL implementations use descending (reverse) order by epoch. 4. Create cross-client test vectors: provide identical BLOB_SCHEDULE data and epoch queries, verify identical parameter outputs. 5. Document any discrepancies between clients that could lead to consensus failures.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-59, Supporting node check for actor-blob-schedule and actor-consensus-layer. Inter-client agreement on sorting is essential for network consensus.

checklist_item.title: Verify Blob Parameter Lookup Logic: Does get_blob_parameters correctly return the first matching entry from descending-sorted schedule?

reachability.notes: Sorting direction is critical for correct schedule lookup. Descending sort finds the most recent applicable entry. Ascending sort would find the earliest entry, returning outdated parameters.

checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Construct a BLOB_SCHEDULE with multiple entries at different epochs (e.g., epochs 100, 200, 300 with distinct blob limits). 2. Query get_blob_parameters at epoch 250 - verify it returns parameters from epoch 200 entry (not 100 or 300). 3. Query at epoch 350 - verify it returns parameters from epoch 300. 4. Query at epoch 99 - verify behavior when no matching entry exists (should fail or return default). 5. Verify the invariant: for any query epoch E, the returned entry's EPOCH is the largest value <= E. 6. Test epoch boundary: query at exactly the epoch value of an entry.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-59, Invariant verification for SG-001. This tests the core logic that determines which blob parameters apply at any given epoch.
